channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration places Christopher Columbus statue on White House grounds
item.description: The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration placed a statue of Christopher Columbus on the White House grounds. Some people are not happy about it.

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Iran war disrupts fertilizer exports just as U.S. farmers begin to plant crops
item.description: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.content:encoded: Gulf states are major fertilizer producers, and the war with Iran has triggered a 25% price hike, just as struggling U.S. farmers are planting corn.
item.dc:creator: Frank Morris

item.title: Israelis' support for the war with Iran remains high, but cynicism is gaining ground
item.description: Israeli support for the war is still high but waning, after three weeks of fighting with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Israeli support for the war is still high but waning, after three weeks of fighting with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Trump said the U.S. is having 'conversations' with Iran. Here's what we know
item.description: After days of brinkmanship, President Trump announces a sudden delay in planned strikes on Iran's power grid, citing "very good and productive" talks aimed at ending the escalating war.
item.content:encoded: After days of brinkmanship, President Trump announces a sudden delay in planned strikes on Iran's power grid, citing "very good and productive" talks aimed at ending the escalating war.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: How the runway crash and ICE agents' arrival are affecting LaGuardia
item.description: As President Trump says he's sending Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents to help with TSA screening at U.S. airports, we hear from frustrated passengers at New York CIty's LaGuardia airport.
item.content:encoded: As President Trump says he's sending Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents to help with TSA screening at U.S. airports, we hear from frustrated passengers at New York CIty's LaGuardia airport.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Denmark goes to the polls in a snap election spurred by Trump's Greenland threat
item.description: Danish voters head to the polls in an election spurred by President Trump's threat to seize Greenland.
item.content:encoded: Danish voters head to the polls in an election spurred by President Trump's threat to seize Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Wisconsin's outgoing Democratic governor pushes for a ban on gerrymandering
item.description: The major political parties' redistricting battle has spread across the country. But Wisconsin may go the other direction. That's if the outgoing governor gets his way.
item.content:encoded: The major political parties' redistricting battle has spread across the country. But Wisconsin may go the other direction. That's if the outgoing governor gets his way.
item.dc:creator: Anya van Wagtendonk

item.title: The secret weapon in 'Project Hail Mary' is Ryan Gosling's star power
item.description: Ryan Gosling stars in a big-screen adaptation of Andy Weir's science-fiction novel Project Hail Mary, directed by Phil Lord and Chris Miller.
item.content:encoded: Ryan Gosling stars in a big-screen adaptation of Andy Weir's science-fiction novel Project Hail Mary, directed by Phil Lord and Chris Miller.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Supreme Court skeptical of laws counting mail-in ballots after election day
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court heard arguments today in a case that tests whether states should be allowed to count ballots that are mailed on time but arrive after Election Day. The case could have broad implications.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: What the war with Iran means for the U.S. relationship with China
item.description: President Trump's agenda with China has been impacted by the U.S.- and Israel-led war in Iran, beyond even just the postponement of his trip.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's agenda with China has been impacted by the U.S.- and Israel-led war in Iran, beyond even just the postponement of his trip.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
